sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: BHP share price tipped to rise to $56: expert
feed.summary: Amid soaring commodity prices, six brokers have re-evaluated their ratings and 12-month price targets for BHP Group Ltd (ASX: BHP). Bank of America's Jason Fairclough stands out, reaffirming a 'buy' rating and increasing his price target from $49 to $56, suggesting an 18% upside. Other analysts from Macquarie, Morgan Stanley, Citi, Barclays, and Ord Minnett also updated their targets, mostly reiterating 'hold' or 'buy' with price increases.

feed.title: Forecasting The Future: 8 Analyst Projections For Sarepta Therapeutics
feed.summary: Eight analysts have provided diverse ratings for Sarepta Therapeutics (NASDAQ: SRPT) over the last three months, with an average 12-month price target of $22.25, a slight increase from the previous average. While the company demonstrates a strong market presence and efficient cost management with a net margin of -45.06%, it faces challenges in revenue growth, return on equity, and return on assets. Its debt-to-equity ratio is also notably higher than the industry average.

feed.title: Up 948%, Should You Buy Sandisk Right Now?
feed.summary: Sandisk (NASDAQ: SNDK) has seen a remarkable 948% surge in its stock price since its spin-off from Western Digital, driven by high demand for flash storage memory due to AI applications. Despite this rally, the company's valuation remains attractive, trading at 7 times sales compared to the U.S. technology sector's average of 8.7. With strong earnings growth expected and increasing demand across data centers, consumer devices, and edge devices, Sandisk appears to be a compelling investment opportunity.

feed.title: Palo Alto, Fortinet stocks are diving. Why they’re the S&P 500’s worst performers today
feed.summary: This article reports that Palo Alto Networks (PANW) and Fortinet (FTNT) are the worst-performing stocks in the S&P 500 today, both experiencing significant declines. This decline follows a downgrade by financial analysts at Morgan Stanley, who also lowered their price targets for both cybersecurity companies. The downgrade is attributed to concerns about increased competition and a potential slowdown in growth in the cybersecurity sector.

feed.title: BD Names Shawn Bevec as Senior Vice President of Investor Relations
feed.summary: BD (Becton, Dickinson and Company) has appointed Shawn Bevec as its new senior vice president of investor relations, effective January 20, 2026. Bevec will be responsible for communicating BD's strategy, performance, and growth opportunities to investors and the financial community. He brings extensive experience from Quest Diagnostics, Deutsche Bank, Susquehanna International Group, Pfizer, and Johnson & Johnson to this role.

feed.title: Labcorp Declares Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: Labcorp Holdings Inc. (NYSE: LH) announced that its Board of Directors has declared a cash dividend of $0.72 per share of common stock. This dividend will be payable on March 12, 2026, to stockholders of record as of February 27, 2026. Labcorp is a global leader in laboratory services, providing diagnostics and drug development support in approximately 100 countries.

feed.title: Boeing delivered 600 aircraft in 2025 and outsold Airbus
feed.summary: Boeing delivered 600 commercial aircraft in 2025, a significant increase from the previous year, and outsold Airbus in terms of new aircraft orders for the first time since 2018. This turnaround indicates a regaining of market confidence in the US planemaker after several years of issues. The company's performance was bolstered by strong sales of its 737 MAX and 787 Dreamliner, and further growth is anticipated with the reintegration of Spirit AeroSystems.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Adjusts Price Target on eBay to $77 From $78, Maintains Sell Rating
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs has adjusted its price target for eBay (EBAY) to $77 from $78, while maintaining a Sell rating on the stock. This report comes alongside other recent analyst adjustments and insider transaction news for the online marketplace company. eBay operates a leading online platform for buying and selling goods and services globally.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Adjusts PT on Expeditors International of Washington to $126 From $119, Keeps Sell Rating

This update comes amidst similar adjustments from other financial institutions, highlighting ongoing analyst evaluations in the air freight and logistics sector. Expeditors International specializes in air and maritime transport services, customs clearance, and other logistics solutions operating globally.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Adjusts PT on C.H. Robinson Worldwide to $149 From $131, Maintains Neutral Rating
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs has increased its price target for C.H. Robinson Worldwide (CHRW) to $149 from $131, while retaining a Neutral rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a revised outlook based on their analysis. The article also lists several other analyst price target changes for C.H. Robinson Worldwide from various firms.

feed.title: MoD and Oracle forge closer ties as IT modernization accelerates
feed.summary: The UK’s Ministry of Defence (MoD) has expanded its agreement with Oracle to accelerate IT modernization, including migrating legacy systems to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI). This partnership aims to streamline data access, enhance security, support AI adoption, and develop digital skills within the MoD, addressing previous challenges highlighted by a 2022 National Audit Office report. The collaboration underscores the MoD's goal to become an "AI-ready organization" and leverage data as a strategic asset for national security.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Profit Misses Estimates as NII, Expenses Fall Short
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Co. missed analysts' profit estimates due to higher severance costs and lower-than-expected net interest income. The bank incurred $612 million in severance as part of a cost-cutting initiative, pushing total expenses to $13.7 billion, exceeding analyst predictions.

feed.title: Microsoft Pledges To Bear Own Data Center Power Costs Amid Trump Pressure Over Surging Energy Bills
feed.summary: Microsoft has committed to covering the power costs for its data centers to prevent local electricity bills from rising, a move made amid growing scrutiny of tech companies' environmental impact and direct pressure from former President Donald Trump. The company plans a "community-first" approach, collaborating with utilities and creating jobs, while also specifically addressing concerns about water usage and the financial burden posed by its AI infrastructure expansion. This pledge follows a contentious data center project in Michigan and reinforces Microsoft's earlier commitment to investing heavily in AI.

feed.title: Is Invesco (IVZ) Starting To Look Expensive After Strong One Year Share Price Gains
feed.summary: Invesco's stock (IVZ) has seen significant gains over the past year (78.2%), leading to questions about its current valuation. While an Excess Returns analysis suggests the stock is heavily overvalued by 242.2%, trading at US$28.30 against an intrinsic value of US$8.27, a Price vs. Earnings comparison indicates it's slightly undervalued with a P/E of 18.82x versus a Fair Ratio of 19.31x. Investors are encouraged to use Simply Wall St's unique "Narratives" tool for a personalized valuation assessment based on individual forecasts.

feed.title: Teradata Included in Gartner Peer Insights™ Voice of the Customer for Data Science and Machine Learning Platforms
feed.summary: Teradata announced its inclusion in the 2025 Gartner Peer Insights™ Voice of the Customer for Data Science and Machine Learning (DSML) Platforms, a recognition based on direct customer feedback. The company received a 95% "Willingness to Recommend" score and a 4.7 out of 5 rating for Product Capabilities. Teradata states this highlights its commitment to delivering an autonomous AI and knowledge platform that helps enterprises operationalize advanced analytics and AI/ML at scale.

feed.title: Petrobras Sets Date for 2026 Annual General Meeting on April 16
feed.summary: Petrobras (PBR) has announced its 2026 Annual General Meeting will be held on April 16, 2026, aligning with its corporate calendar. The specific details regarding the meeting's time, location, and format will be disclosed later. Despite a Hold rating from analysts and some bearish technical momentum, TipRanks' AI Analyst "Spark" rates PBR as Neutral, citing strong financial performance, an attractive valuation, and high dividend yield against short-term risks.

feed.title: Coca-Cola reportedly abandons plans to sell Costa Coffee chain
feed.summary: Coca-Cola has reportedly halted plans to sell its Costa Coffee chain after bids from private equity firms failed to meet its £2bn sale expectations. The company had initially purchased Costa for £3.9bn in 2018 but the chain has since struggled with rising costs and competition, leading to widened operating losses. While the immediate sale is off, Coca-Cola has not ruled out future divestment.
